# RQ1 — SEAMLESS ep1000 (train 1000 + frozen tail 200; memory_size unchanged)

**Auto-generated by `deploy_seamless1000_driver.ps1` -> `analyze_seamless1000.py`** (detached). NUMBERS ONLY; operator cross-checks raw `.mat`. Idea 1: does more training (with the buffer unchanged) push the cost-critic loss lower then flat, while violation stays at eps?

## 1. Acceptance gate — first-600 episodes reproduce Canonical_ep600

(AoI_evolution is a rolling last-100ep window; for ep1000 that is ep900-999, so we compare viol_rate[:, :600] / lambda[:, :600] / AoI[:, :600].)

| arm | seed | viol_rate[:600] | lambda[:600] | AoI[:600] |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| soft | 2 | YES | YES | YES |
| soft | 3 | YES | YES | YES |
| soft | 4 | YES | YES | YES |
| soft | 5 | YES | YES | YES |
| soft | 6 | YES | YES | YES |
| soft | 7 | YES | YES | YES |
| pid | 2 | YES | YES | YES |
| pid | 3 | YES | YES | YES |
| pid | 4 | YES | YES | YES |
| pid | 5 | YES | YES | YES |
| pid | 6 | YES | YES | YES |
| pid | 7 | YES | YES | YES |

**GATE: ALL PASS**

## 2. Buffer-eviction hypothesis (hard, net-mean over 5 platoons)

| seed | critic_loss [50:150] | [400:500] | [500:600] | [900:1000] || reward_cost(viol) [400:500] | [500:600] | [900:1000] |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 2 | 0.863 | 0.669 | 0.388 | 0.189 || 0.108 | 0.099 | 0.096 |
| 3 | 0.795 | 0.597 | 0.415 | 0.556 || 0.117 | 0.115 | 0.079 |
| 4 | 0.547 | 0.427 | 0.280 | 0.352 || 0.087 | 0.099 | 0.085 |
| 5 | 0.659 | 0.613 | 0.374 | 0.365 || 0.090 | 0.101 | 0.089 |
| 6 | 0.748 | 0.505 | 0.312 | 0.349 || 0.091 | 0.095 | 0.153 |
| 7 | 0.823 | 0.637 | 0.372 | 0.327 || 0.103 | 0.080 | 0.096 |

(prediction: critic_loss [900:1000] <= [500:600] <= [400:500] then flat; reward_cost ~ eps=0.10 throughout.)
